title: inline polli-cli readme into api reference
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Mirrors how BYOP is included in the API reference: source the full README via a Vite raw import instead of duplicating a handwritten snippet in docs.ts.

- adds `import CLI_README from "../../../packages/polli-cli/README.md?raw"`
- strips the leading h1 and the github user-attachments video line at inline time so the README stays as-is on GitHub
- removes the hardcoded `CLI_DOCS` block

Net: -14/+5 lines, single source of truth for CLI docs.

gist.headline: Single source of truth
gist.blurb: The API reference now pulls the polli-cli documentation directly from its source. No more out-of-sync instructions!
gist.summary: The API reference now dynamically imports the full polli-cli README via Vite instead of relying on a hardcoded snippet.
gist.impact: Developers reading the API docs will always see the most up-to-date command-line tool instructions directly from the source repository.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing documents, taking a single glowing master scroll and placing it into a larger reference book, representing the 'single source of truth' for documentation.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing tiny reading glasses, carefully placing a glowing master scroll into a large, open reference book. A chunky retro monitor nearby displays clean text. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
